title: Error Handling in Node.js Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of synchronous vs asynchronous strategies, Express error middleware (4-argument handlers), and global crash prevention.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Error Handling' in a Node.js application?
options: Catching and managing exceptions to prevent the server from crashing and to provide professional feedback to users.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: In Express.js, how many arguments should an 'Error Middleware' function have?
Which modern JavaScript keyword is used inside an 'async' function to catch errors in a line-by-line way?
Which global Node.js event would you use to catch an error that wasn't caught elsewhere before it crashes the server?
Does following a solid 'Error Handling' strategy help build truly Fullstack applications by ensuring high availability and professional user feedback?
options: Yes, as it is a core feature of any modern production web application.
